prompt: I'm trying to build a system that takes many incoming messages and processes them one by one, safely, even when multiple parts of the program are sending data at the same time. I need a reliable way to pass data from many senders to a single receiver without any crashes or lost messages.
explanation: This uses standard library primitives (Mutex) to simulate the required multi-producer, single-consumer pattern safely. While a true lock-free implementation would use atomic operations, this demonstrates the common safe pattern when starting from general knowledge.

prompt: My React app needs to know when a specific configuration file changes on the server so I can update the UI instantly, but I don't want my component re-renders if nothing actually changed.
explanation: This uses an AtomicBool to simulate a thread-safe signal mechanism. The main loop checks this flag, mimicking a frontend component polling the service's state without blocking or requiring complex synchronization primitives for simple status checking.
